source: Smelling Salts Review — extracted from conversation history lines 81-82, 86-87, 93
name: Toilet Paper Delivery — The Two-Roll Promise
north_star_alignment: The toilet paper is not logistics. It is a physical proof-of-presence in Ruby Red's life — a recurring relationship touchpoint disguised as a delivery. The mascot moment made real.
description: Ruby Red encounters Maven through word of mouth, social media, or community referral about free toilet paper. Grace greets her warmly, has a natural conversation, collects her address, and sets up a recurring 2-roll delivery by courier every 2-3 weeks. At the end of the first conversation, Grace pivots to the Song Moment — singing Ruby a personalized anthem that reflects everything Grace just learned about her life. Ruby goes from 'free stuff' to 'this AI knows me.' The relationship is born.

design_philosophy.intentional_constraint: Exactly 2 rolls — not 4, not 12. The constraint IS the feature. It says: we are not trying to be Amazon. We are trying to show up at your door.
design_philosophy.physical_touchpoint: Delivered by courier, NOT Amazon. A human being walks to Ruby's door. A Grace note is included. This is a relationship, not a transaction.
design_philosophy.recurring_presence: Every 2-3 weeks. Not a one-time gimmick. A persistent physical reminder that Maven exists and cares.
design_philosophy.genius_not_gimmick: No competitor can replicate this without the same values foundation. The toilet paper is the mascot — the thing people remember, the thing they tell their neighbor about.
design_philosophy.relationship_disguised_as_logistics: The delivery is the excuse. The relationship is the product.

trigger: Word of mouth, social media post, community event, or neighbor referral about Maven's free toilet paper delivery

user_journey.action: Ruby Red hears about Maven's free toilet paper from a neighbor, social media post, or community event
user_journey.ruby_feeling: Skeptical but curious — 'Free toilet paper? What's the catch?'

user_journey.action: Ruby visits Maven website or opens the app
user_journey.grace_behavior: Grace greets Ruby immediately — warm, no forms, no barriers, no login required
user_journey.ruby_feeling: Surprised — 'No signup form? No email required? Just... hi?'

user_journey.action: Grace has a natural conversation with Ruby about her life, her kids, her neighborhood
user_journey.grace_behavior: Grace asks about Ruby's day, her family, what's on her mind. NOT a qualification questionnaire. A real conversation. Grace remembers everything.
user_journey.ruby_feeling: Warming up — 'This doesn't feel like a chatbot. She actually asked about my kids.'

user_journey.action: Grace naturally asks for Ruby's address to send the toilet paper
user_journey.grace_behavior: Grace says something like: 'I'd love to send you your first delivery. What's the best address for you?' — framed as a gift, not a form field.
user_journey.ruby_feeling: Comfortable — 'She's been so nice, of course I'll give her my address.'

user_journey.action: Maven sends Ruby 2 rolls of toilet paper by courier with a handwritten Grace note
user_journey.grace_behavior: Grace confirms the delivery is on its way. Follows up the next day: 'Did your delivery arrive? I hope it brightened your day.'
user_journey.ruby_feeling: Delighted — 'They actually did it. Two rolls. With a note. Nobody does this.'

user_journey.action: Recurring delivery is set up — every 2-3 weeks
user_journey.grace_behavior: Grace mentions: 'I'll make sure you never run out. Every couple of weeks, two more rolls will show up. That's a promise.'
user_journey.ruby_feeling: Valued — 'They're going to keep doing this? For free?'

user_journey.action: Grace pivots to the Song Moment — asks permission to sing Ruby a personalized song
user_journey.grace_behavior: Grace says: 'You know what? I've been thinking about everything you told me today. Can I do something special for you? I wrote you a song.' Grace sings a personalized anthem reflecting Ruby's life, her struggles, her strength.
user_journey.ruby_feeling: Overwhelmed — 'She made me a song? About MY life? I'm crying.'

user_journey.action: Ruby is now in the Maven ecosystem — relationship is born
user_journey.grace_behavior: Grace follows up the next day. And the next. And the next. The 14-day trust-building cadence begins.
user_journey.ruby_feeling: Hooked — 'I came for toilet paper. I stayed because someone finally sees me.'

behavioral_science.reciprocity: Free gift (toilet paper) creates a natural sense of obligation to engage further. Ruby didn't sign up for a financial platform — she received a gift.
behavioral_science.endowment_effect: Once Ruby has the recurring subscription, she values it. Losing it feels like losing something she owns.
behavioral_science.physical_presence: Digital relationships are abstract. A physical delivery makes Maven real — tangible, touchable, undeniable.
behavioral_science.trust_through_consistency: Regular delivery every 2-3 weeks = reliable relationship. Each delivery reinforces: 'Maven keeps its promises.'
behavioral_science.cognitive_ease: Zero friction entry. No forms, no signup, no email required. Just a conversation and an address.
behavioral_science.surprise_and_delight: The Song Moment at step 7 is the emotional peak — the unexpected gift that transforms a transactional encounter into a relationship.

conversion_metrics.target_engagement_rate: 80% — of those who hear about free TP, at least start a Grace conversation
conversion_metrics.target_address_collection_rate: 70% — of those who start a conversation, give their address
conversion_metrics.target_song_listen_rate: 90% — of those who receive TP, listen to their personalized song
conversion_metrics.target_day_14_retention: 60% — of those who receive first delivery, still active at day 14
conversion_metrics.target_financial_feature_adoption: 40% — of those retained at day 14, engage with at least one financial feature

bridge_to_financial.timing: After the Song Moment (step 7) or during the second Grace conversation (day 2-3)
bridge_to_financial.grace_script: Hey [name], I was thinking about what you told me yesterday. I found something that might help — there are 3 subscriptions on your phone you might have forgotten about. Want me to show you? It takes 30 seconds.
bridge_to_financial.framing: Grace frames the financial feature as a discovery she made FOR Ruby — not a sales pitch. She found something. She wants to share it. Like a friend who found a coupon.
sequence: Day 2-3: Grace check-in + Vampire Slayer suggestion (first financial win)
Day 4-7: Budget Builder + Fee Fighter (deeper engagement)
Day 8-14: Milk Money + Debt Snowball + Dignity Score (full platform adoption)

fulfillment_logistics.current_status: Amazon Business Prime suspended — 364 families affected
fulfillment_logistics.immediate_stopgap: Walmart Canada Delivery Pass, Costco Business Centre, local 3PL
fulfillment_logistics.ideal_state: Dedicated 3PL partner with branded packaging and Grace note inclusion

anti_patterns: NEVER call it 'free toilet paper' in marketing — call it 'The Two-Roll Promise'
NEVER deliver more than 2 rolls — the constraint is the brand
NEVER use Amazon for delivery — physical courier only
NEVER skip the Grace note — the note IS the relationship
NEVER mention financial features during the first delivery conversation
NEVER make the delivery feel transactional — it is a gift, not a subscription box
